data.title: China firmly opposes new U.S. tariffs, vows retaliation
data.description:
China has strongly condemned the new tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump, saying that it "firmly opposes" the measures and will implement countermeasures to protect its interests.

Trump on Wednesday said that China will be hit by 34% tariffs on top of the 20% he announced earlier, bringing the total levies on Chinese goods to 54%.  Additionally, Trump signed an executive order to close the "de minimis" trade loophole, which has permitted duty-free entry of low-value shipments from Hong Kong and China into the U.S.

Beijing urged Washington to immediately cancel the tariffs, warning that they endanger global economic development, disrupt supply chains, and even harm U.S. businesses.

The Chinese Commerce Ministry said that the tariffs "do not comply with international trade rules and seriously harm the legitimate rights and interests of the relevant parties."

"The US claims to have suffered losses in international trade, using so-called 'reciprocity' as an excuse to raise tariffs on all trade partners," Beijing added.

China has called for "dialogue" to resolve the dispute. "There is no winner in a trade war, and there is no way out for protectionism," it said, adding that "history has proven that raising tariffs does not solve the US's own problems."

In response, China plans retaliatory measures, levying up to 15% on U.S. agricultural products such as soybeans, pork, and chicken. These countermeasures aim to safeguard China's interests amidst an escalating trade war that threatens its fragile economic recovery.

 

China ETFs: (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:FLCH), (BATS:CNYA), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), and (NYSEARCA:YINN).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Stellantis to pause Canada's Windsor plant for two weeks due to tariffs, union says
data.description:
• Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) will close its assembly plant in Windsor, Ontario for two weeks, Canadian labour union Unifor said on Wednesday.
  • The union posted a message to its Facebook page relaying company plans to be “down for two weeks” beginning April 7.
  • “While we had heard rumours of potential downtime, the company said there are multiple factors at play, with the primary driver behind the final decision being this afternoon’s announcement from U.S. President Donald Trump of the U.S. tariffs,” the post by local president, James Stewart said.
  • While Canada has been spared from reciprocal tariffs, the Trump administration maintains 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum as well as newly placed 25% tariffs on all foreign-made automobiles.
  • More European equities ETFs: (IEV), (IEUR), (STOXX), (FEZ)

data.title: Australia's trade surplus shrinks to 2020 low; imports surge, exports fall
data.description:
The Australian trade surplus in February 2025 drastically undershot market predictions, settling at AUD 2.97 billion, well below the anticipated AUD 5.6 billion. This represents the smallest surplus since August 2020, as exports decreased while imports increased, following a revised January surplus of AUD 5.16 billion.

Australia's goods exports fell 3.6% from a month earlier to a four-month low of AUD 42.31 billion in February 2025, reversing from a downwardly revised 0.8% rise in January.

Australia's imports of goods increased by 1.6% month-over-month to a record high of AUD 39.34 billion in February 2025, reversing a revised 0.4% deline in the previous month. 

The S&P/ASX 200 Index climbed 0.4% on Tuesday, surpassing 7,870. The Australian dollar hovered near $0.63 on Thursday.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWA), (NYSEARCA:FXA), (NYSEARCA:FLAU).

More on Australia

  • Australia’s monthly CPI eased to three-month low of 2.4% in February
  • Australia's Feb unemployment rate remains unchanged at 4.1%, aligning with expectations
  • Australia’s trade balance exceeds expectations, export growth accelerates and imports decline
  • RBA cuts its cash rate by 25 bps for first time since 2020, in-line with market expectations
  • Australia's manufacturing downturn deepens in December

data.title: Indian official calls Trump's 26% reciprocal tariff ‘mixed bag, not a setback’
data.description:
The Trump administration has slapped 26% reciprocal tariffs on India, labelling them discounted.

The levies are lower than other Asian countries like China, Vietnam, Indonesia and Thailand.

However, the levies on India were higher compared to Japan (24%), South Korea (25%), and Malaysia (24%).

The U.S. has imposed 37% on Bangladesh, 44% on Sri Lanka and 29% on Pakistan.

“India charges us 52%, so we will charge them half of that – 26%,” the U.S. President said, as he  unveiled reciprocal tariffs on countries that levy duties on American goods.

A commerce ministry official told news agency PTI that the impact was a “mixed bag and not a setback”.

The official, who wished anonymity, said the universal 10% tariffs will come into effect on all imports into the U.S. from April 5 and the remaining 16% from April 10. “It is a mixed bag and not a setback for India,” the official said.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

ETFs to track the S&P (SP500): (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

data.title: Japan's Mar services PMI revised higher to 50; yen rallies amid trade war concerns
data.description:
The Au Jibun Bank Japan Services PMI recorded 50 in March 2025, improving from the flash estimate of 49.5 but declining from February’s six-month high of 53.7. 

The Au Jibun Bank of Japan Composite PMI stood at 48.9 in March 2025, slightly above the flash reading of 48.5 but down from 52.0 in February, and marks the first decline in private sector activity since October and the steepest drop since November 2022. 

In response to the new U.S. tariffs, Japan expressed 'serious concerns,' labeled the decision 'extremely regrettable,' and is exploring all counter-measures. They requested an exemption, formed a task force to assess the impact, and raised WTO compliance concerns, while facing a separate 25% car tariff.

The Nikkei 225 Index tumbled 4% to drop below 35,000, while the Topix Index slid 3.3% to 2,560 on Thursday, as Japanese stocks sank to multi-month lows following US President Donald Trump’s sweeping reciprocal tariffs. The Japanese yen surged toward 147 per dollar on Thursday, hitting a three-week high

More on Japan

  • Japan’s unemployment rate unexpectedly drops to 2.4% in February

  • Japan's Feb industrial production exceeds expectations, retail sales fall short of forecasts

  • Japan’s manufacturing downturn extends to ninth month; services activity turns negative

  • Japan’s core inflation rate slows to 3% in February, exceeds forecast; food prices slow slightly

  • Japan’s trade balance swung to surplus in February, driven by strong exports

data.title: China's services growth hits 3-month high; yuan falls on Trump's aggressive tariffs
data.description:
The Caixin China General Services PMI increased to 51.9 in March 2025, up from 51.4 in the previous month, surpassing market forecasts of 51.6.

This growth was the strongest since December, driven by increased domestic demand, marketing efforts, and improved demand conditions.

The Caixin China General Composite PMI rose to 51.8 in March 2025 from 51.5 in February, marking its highest level since last November, marking the 17th consecutive month of growth in private sector activity, driven by the strongest expansion in manufacturing output in four months and a three-month peak in service sector growth.

Separately, the trade dispute between China and the U.S. intensified as Beijing announced plans for "resolute" countermeasures following the Trump administration's imposition of further tariffs, bringing the total to 54% on certain Chinese imports. China's Commerce Ministry called for the U.S. to reverse its unilateral tariffs and pursue dialogue, while simultaneously denouncing the measures as violations of international trade rules and acts of "unilateral bullying."

Traders now await Beijing’s response to Trump’s latest move.

The Shanghai Composite fell 0.3% to around 3,340 while the Shenzhen Component dropped 0.6% to 10,450 on Thursday, with mainland stocks snapping a two-day advance as US President Donald Trump imposed a 34% reciprocal tariff on China, intensifying the trade conflict between the world’s two largest economies, and the offshore yuan depreciated toward 7.32 per dollar on Thursday, touching a one-month low in the earlier session.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (NYSEARCA:YINN), (NYSE:TDF), (NYSEARCA:CHIQ), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:EWH), (NYSEARCA:KBA), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NASDAQ:CXSE), (NYSE:CAF), (NYSEARCA:CWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:KURE).

Currency: (CNY:USD)

More on China markets and economy

  • China government debuts green sovereign bond offering

  • China’s Mar manufacturing activity accelerates to four-month high amid robust external demand

  • China’s factory activity expands at fastest pace in March, services sector growth hits three-month high

  • China’s retail sales accelerate 4.0% Y/Y; industrial output grow 5.9% y/y exceeds forecasts; unveils special action plan

  • China’s CPI fell for first time in 13 months, PPI deflation persists

data.title: Thursday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
7:30 AM Challenger Job-Cut Report

This monthly report counts and categorizes announcements of corporate layoffs based on mass layoff data from state departments.

8:30 AM International Trade in Goods and Services

Updating the goods portion of the advance report and offering initial data on services, this report provides complete information on cross-border trade. Forecasters look for goods & services trade gap at $122.7 billion in February versus $131.4 billion in January.

8:30 AM Jobless Claims

New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time. Claims have been holding surprisingly steady around 225K and are expected at 226K in the latest week.

9:45 AM PMI Composite Final

The Composite Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) provides an estimate of private sector output for the preceding month by combining information obtained from surveys of around 750 manufacturing and service sector companies.

10:00 AM ISM Services Index

Producing a monthly composite on general activity tracked in volumes, the Institute for Supply Management surveys several hundred service-providing firms from 16 industries. Another decent month of expansion seen in March with the index at 53.0 versus 53.5 in February.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on natural gas stocks in underground storage for the U.S. and five regions of the country. The level of inventories helps determine prices for natural gas products.

12:30 PM Fed's Jefferson Speech

Federal Reserve Vice Chair Philip Jefferson will speak on"U.S. Economic Outlook and Central Bank Communications" before the Conference on Financial Intermediaries, Markets and Monetary Policy hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta and the University of Virginia.

2:30 PM Fed's Cook Speech

Federal Reserve Board Governor Lisa Cook will speak on the economic outlook before the University of Pittsburgh 2025 McKay Lecture event.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed balance sheet is a weekly report listing the assets and liabilities of the Federal Reserve System.

data.title: RH projects 10% to 13% revenue growth for fiscal 2025 with strategic product expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: RH (RH) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Gary Friedman highlighted a revenue increase of 18% and a 57% growth in adjusted operating income during Q4 2024. He credited the results to the company’s product transformation and platform expansion, which he described as the most significant in the industry.
  • RH brand demand increased 21% in Q4, driven by new product offerings. The company launched new collections in its RH Outdoor and RH Interiors Sourcebooks, featuring expanded furniture assortments and improved in-stock positions.
  • The company plans to introduce a new brand extension in fall 2025, including Sourcebooks, a dedicated website presence, and two freestanding galleries.
  • CFO Jack Preston emphasized the company’s focus on leveraging its inventory position, which is expected to support strategic flexibility amid market uncertainties.

Outlook

  • RH forecasts fiscal 2025 revenue growth between 10% and 13%, an adjusted operating margin of 14% to 15%, and an adjusted EBITDA margin of 20% to 21%.
  • For Q1 2025, the company projects revenue growth of 12.5% to 13.5%, with an adjusted operating margin of 6.5% to 7% and an adjusted EBITDA margin of 12.5% to 13%.
  • Management noted that start-up costs for international expansion will impact operating margins by 160 to 200 basis points in fiscal 2025.

Financial Results

  • The company reported Q4 revenue growth of 18% and adjusted operating income growth of 57%. Demand in January stabilized at 19% growth following volatility in December due to rising mortgage rates.
  • The company ended the year with excess inventory valued at $200 million to $300 million at cost, which it intends to convert into cash through assortment optimization.
  • RH highlighted its significant investments in real estate, estimating an equity value of $500 million in owned properties, which may be monetized as market conditions warrant.

Q&A

  • Steven Forbes, Guggenheim: Asked about inventory strategy in light of tariff developments. CEO Friedman explained that the company had proactively invested in inventory as part of its product transformation and noted flexibility in navigating tariff impacts.
  • Max Rakhlenko, TD Cowen: Inquired about peak demand growth. CEO Friedman indicated that the company’s product transformation and upcoming brand extension suggest several years of sustained growth potential.
  • Andrew Carter, Stifel: Sought clarification on tariff impacts and supply chain adjustments. CEO Friedman stated that RH is evaluating its sourcing strategy but anticipates navigating challenges effectively due to its scale and partnerships.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on RH's ability to mitigate tariff impacts and maintain growth momentum. Questions reflected concerns about inventory levels and broader economic challenges.
  • Management's tone was confident, emphasizing strategic flexibility and long-term growth opportunities. CEO Friedman frequently referenced the company’s resilience and innovation capabilities, using phrases like “we thrive in times of uncertainty.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management exhibited a similar level of confidence but provided more details on mitigating macroeconomic risks.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 2024 guidance reflects an increase in revenue growth expectations compared to Q3, where demand growth trends were also strong but at a lower scale.
  • Management's tone remained consistent, focusing on strategic investments and long-term growth.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted slightly from overall market conditions in Q3 to the impact of tariffs and inventory levels in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified tariffs, market volatility, and inflation risks as key challenges. The company plans to mitigate these through its sourcing strategy and inventory management.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the potential impact of tariffs on margins and the timing of demand realization from new product introductions.

Final Takeaway

RH’s Q4 2024 earnings call highlighted a strong performance driven by its ongoing product transformation and strategic platform expansion. The company provided robust guidance for fiscal 2025, projecting revenue growth of 10% to 13% and maintaining its focus on long-term market share gains through innovative product launches and global expansion. Management conveyed confidence in navigating external challenges, including tariffs and market volatility, supported by a solid inventory position and strategic flexibility.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Tesla bulls look past DOGE backlash and the deliveries slowdown to see a bright autonomy/AI future
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) fell more than 8% in postmarket trading on Wednesday to erase a solid gain of 5.3% during the regular session. Early in the day, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) reported a sharp drop in Q1 deliveries due to lost production from the Model Y refresh and potential backlash against Elon Musk for his role with the Trump Administration.

Regarding Musk's status, the Trump administration pushed back on earlier reports that Elon Musk would leave the administration in the coming weeks. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt wrote on social media site X that "Elon will depart from public service as a special government employee when his incredible work at DOGE is complete."

Even the biggest Tesla (TSLA) bulls on the Street have wavered about the path ahead. Morgan Stanley analyst Adam Jonas said Tesla (TSLA) will be put to the test to see if they can outperform despite falling estimates and with the key autonomous driving test starting in June in Austin. "Our OW rating and $410 price target ($800 bull case, $200 bear case) are underpinned by our belief that Tesla's capabilities in key areas of physical AI (AVs, humanoids and other form factors) including data, robotics, energy storage, compute, manufacturing and space/comms/networking/infrastructure offer growth and margin opportunities that greatly exceed those of the traditional EV business which is under pressure," wrote Jonas on why the firm is still confident.

Meanwhile, Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives highlighted that Tesla (TSLA) has unfortunately become a political symbol because of Elon Musk, which is seen as a bad thing for the future of the technology stalwart. "Musk needs to stop this political firestorm and balance being CEO of Tesla with DOGE. The future is so bright but this is a full-blown crisis Tesla is navigating now (along with these head scratching tariffs) and it's primarily self-inflected by Musk," he advised. Despite the dizzying stream of negative news, Ives and his team remain firmly bullish on the long-term Tesla (TSLA) story and maintained an Outperform rating.

On Seeking Alpha, The Asian Investor said Tesla (TSLA) is still a Strong Buy. The view is that more upside could be realized if Tesla (TSLA) manages to ramp up Model 3 deliveries throughout the year and sentiment swings the other way.

data.title: Resources connection sees Q4 revenue at $132M-$137M amid focus on operational efficiency and global growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Resources Connection, Inc. (RGP) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Kate Duchene highlighted the company achieved Q3 revenue of $129.4 million, aligning with expectations despite macroeconomic uncertainties, client budget constraints, and slower project ramp-ups. Gross margin and SG&A expenses were favorable compared to outlook ranges.

  • Management noted significant momentum in Europe, Japan, and the Philippines, with Europe seeing bill rate increases, pipeline expansion, and $1 million-plus project pursuits. Similar growth was observed in Southeast Asia and India.

  • CEO Duchene emphasized progress in diversifying service offerings, particularly targeting CFO services, digital technology, data, and operational performance. Notable advancements included cloud migration support for ERP systems and ServiceNow optimizations.

  • COO Bhadresh Patel shared that the consulting segment saw double-digit bill rate improvements, a 20% increase in enterprise engagement sizes, and doubled the number of $1 million-plus engagements year-over-year. The pipeline of $5 million-plus opportunities has also grown significantly.

  • CFO Jenn Ryu disclosed a $42 million non-cash goodwill impairment charge in Q3, reflecting weaker demand in the on-demand and consulting segments. Despite this, the balance sheet remains strong with $73 million in cash and no debt.

Outlook

  • Management provided Q4 revenue guidance between $132 million to $137 million. Gross margin is expected to normalize between 36% and 37%, while SG&A expenses are projected at $45 million to $47 million.

  • Early Q4 revenue trends indicate some deceleration from Q3 levels, attributed to macroeconomic uncertainties and delays in client decision-making.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q3 2025 amounted to $129.4 million, within the stated outlook range. Gross margin was 35.1%, driven by improvements in average bill rates, which reached $124, up from $119 in the prior year.

  • The consulting segment generated $52.6 million in revenue, reflecting a slight drop compared to the prior year but supported by a 13% improvement in bill rates year-over-year.

  • On-demand segment revenue was $47.1 million, down from the prior year, with adjusted EBITDA of 5%. Outsourced services achieved $9.4 million in revenue, showing stability compared to the prior quarter.

  • The company reported a $42 million non-cash goodwill impairment charge, split between the on-demand and consulting segments.

Q&A

  • Joe Gomes, Noble Capital: Questioned the delays in client decision-making and its impact on high-priority projects. CEO Duchene explained that budget constraints and market uncertainties contributed to these delays, particularly in the U.S., while Europe showed improvement due to pent-up demand.

  • Mark Marcon, Baird: Inquired about project cancellations and delays. CEO Duchene clarified that delays, rather than cancellations, dominate the current environment, with extensions growing in Europe.

  • Andrew Steinerman, JPMorgan: Asked about the year-over-year revenue decline in Q4 guidance. CFO Ryu estimated an organic constant currency decline of 17%, excluding Reference Point acquisition impacts.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns over delays in project starts and macroeconomic uncertainties, reflecting skepticism about near-term revenue growth. Questions focused on the sustainability of current strategies and cost control measures.

  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, emphasizing ongoing efficiency improvements, diversified service offerings, and strong client relationships. CEO Duchene highlighted efforts to position the company for growth once market conditions improve.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s sentiment remained steady, though they acknowledged heightened uncertainty in the U.S. market.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue guidance for Q4 shows a slightly improved range compared to Q3 actuals, reflecting cautious optimism despite ongoing macroeconomic challenges.

  • Gross margin guidance for Q4 (36%-37%) indicates an expected improvement over Q3’s 35.1%, driven by normalization after holiday impacts.

  • Management’s tone on strategic initiatives remained consistent, with a continued focus on CFO services and digital transformation. Analysts’ questions this quarter focused more on delays and macroeconomic impacts compared to prior discussions about operational execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted ongoing macroeconomic uncertainties, including tariff impacts and slower client decision-making, as key challenges to revenue growth.

  • The $42 million goodwill impairment charge reflects continued sluggish demand in the on-demand and consulting segments.

  • Analysts raised concerns about the sustainability of dividends and potential limitations on further cost reductions without impacting operational capabilities.

Final Takeaway

Resources Connection, Inc. continues to navigate a challenging macroeconomic environment with a focus on operational efficiency and service diversification. While Q3 results aligned with expectations, delays in client decision-making and budget constraints remain headwinds. Management remains optimistic about long-term opportunities, supported by a strong balance sheet and strategic positioning across key markets.
